description: Previously froze ~7,800 back-office roles due to AI (May 2023). Now tripling U.S. entry-level hiring for 2026, but for fundamentally different roles. New hires manage chatbots, oversee AI outputs, and intervene when AI fails. CHRO Nickle LaMoreaux: 'The entry-level jobs from two to three years ago? AI can do most of them.' CEO Krishna (Oct 2025): 'We are probably going to hire more people out of college over the next 12 months than we have in the past few years.'
reasonGiven: Tripling entry-level hiring — but for AI-management roles, not traditional back-office work.
jobRolesCreated: Software developers, HR (AI oversight), customer-facing roles — redefined as AI-management positions
context: Reversal from May 2023 hiring freeze. New roles fundamentally different — managing chatbots, overseeing AI outputs, intervening when AI fails.
